types: from pymysql.err import MySQLError, IntegrityError, OperationalError

primary_operations.name: save
primary_operations.sdk_call: with self._conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute('REPLACE INTO entities(id, payload) VALUES (%s, %s)', (entity_id, payload)); self._conn.commit()

primary_operations.name: find_by_id
primary_operations.sdk_call: with self._conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute('SELECT payload FROM entities WHERE id = %s', (entity_id,)); row = cur.fetchone(); return bytes(row[0])

primary_operations.name: delete
primary_operations.sdk_call: with self._conn.cursor() as cur: cur.execute('DELETE FROM entities WHERE id = %s', (entity_id,)); self._conn.commit()

code_style_notes: PyMySQL connections are NOT thread-safe; one connection per thread (or use DBUtils PooledDB)
REPLACE INTO is MySQL-specific upsert syntax; for portability use INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
use %s as the parameter placeholder regardless of underlying column type
